prompt: Create a vertical, photorealistic night festival portrait of an adult Japanese woman standing in a lively summer ennichi street market, shot from mid-thigh upward with a shallow depth of field and warm cinematic bokeh. She wears a pale light lavender-white yukata with pink, blue, and purple cherry-blossom floral print, a wide rose-pink obi sash tied at the back with a visible bow, plus a decorative front cord and flower knot. Her hair is dark brown to black, styled in two low braids/pigtails over the shoulders, with exactly three visible floral hair accessories: one large pink blossom cluster on the right side of her head, one small pink flower cluster near the left shoulder braid, and one small white flower cluster on the right braid. Pose her smiling and playfully lifting the left sleeve outward with her left hand while her right arm extends down and slightly out to the side; emphasize the flowing sleeves, fitted waist, and traditional fabric texture. Place her beside a goldfish-scooping stall on the left with glass or plastic water tanks and trays, glowing paper lanterns, and a large Japanese stall sign reading 金魚すくい; use warm orange lantern light on the skin and fabric, with blurred festival stalls and a walking woman in the background on the right. Composition: subject centered slightly right, close portrait perspective, 3:4 aspect ratio, realistic DSLR photography, 50mm lens look, f/1.8, high detail, natural skin tones, soft highlights, no watermark, no extra text beyond the stall signage.
